items: Quiet update notice replaces intrusive first-viewport update toast.
Actual 460-item look/code review is wired into the in-app improvement drawer.
Sequential workspace smoke command added to avoid visual-smoke browser contention.
Visual smoke manifest now includes screenshot labels.
Shared route error and loading components added.
Typed localStorage helper added with schema validation and corrupted-entry recovery.
Route-to-owner registry added with dependencies, smoke text, and escalation path.
Route registry is included in diagnostic bundles.
Improvement drawer shows owner, dependencies, smoke text, and escalation path.
Hidden page titles added from the shell for routes that lacked H1 coverage.
Command palette has a first-class Actions group for task, note, draft, and agent-job workflows.
Command palette quick actions have desktop keyboard shortcuts with editable-field guards.
Shared source freshness formatter added for Dashboard and Phone Cockpit.
Visual smoke supports route-specific visible selector assertions.
Command palette includes direct actions for common Settings sections.
Visual smoke retries transient runtime navigation and stale-asset recovery redirects.
Smoke commands preflight LaunchAgent/client asset synchronization before route and visual checks.
Route smoke retries transient navigation timeouts with a fresh page before failing a route.
Route smoke auth now backs off on local 429 rate limits instead of running unauthenticated page checks.
Update notice no longer covers the dashboard header.
Gateway stale state promoted to a visible incident/recovery banner.
Visible Dashboard action queue heading added.
Stale overview copy now points to live-evidence refresh before acting.
Dashboard freshness and stale checks now use the shared workspace freshness utility.
Dashboard now computes a single ranked next action from blockers, stale sources, or the current workstream.
Dashboard action queue now has a compact command strip for running the next action.
Dashboard command strip now exposes hidden-widget recovery without requiring users to discover layout edit mode.
Dashboard command strip can copy a safe diagnostics bundle with health, stale state, freshness, hidden widgets, degraded sources, and severity map.
Dashboard action cards now include inline severity chips for faster scanning.
Dashboard diagnostics explicitly exclude secrets and tokens.
Dashboard command-center scoring, status brief, next-action ranking, diagnostics, and workflow presets now live in a route-private pure utility module.
Dashboard command strip now lives in a route-private component module instead of the main route file.
Dashboard helper tests now import pure command-center logic directly instead of importing the full route component.
Dashboard route file is reduced from 2876 to 2633 lines while preserving the current command-center behavior.
Dashboard shared palette, card shell, unavailable widget, time/number formatting, color alpha, and secondary action primitives now live in a route-private UI module.
Dashboard route file is reduced from 2633 to 2444 lines while preserving current first-viewport, command strip, session, and widget behavior.
Dashboard UI primitive extraction keeps the shared source-freshness footer and icon-button semantics consistent across existing route cards.
Update notice no longer covers the urgent-task headline.
Duplicate hidden H1 removed.
Loading state no longer becomes the H1 text.
Additional bottom safe-area spacing protects pinned actions from bottom navigation.
Queued captures now use typed storage validation.
Collapsed card preferences now use typed storage validation.
Pinned card preferences now use typed storage validation.
Pin and collapse controls are icon-only touch targets with accessible labels.
Phone freshness labels now use the shared workspace freshness utility.
Phone tabs, card preference helpers, mode readouts, at-a-glance summaries, signal rail, daily loops, quick logs, freshness notices, and queue readouts now live in a route-private helper module.
Phone helper tests import the pure helper module directly instead of the full route component.
Phone route file is reduced from 3017 to 2335 lines while preserving current daily-mode behavior.
Chat receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Update notice no longer competes with the chat status strip.
Chat workflow panel now has a first-run command rail for task capture, knowledge note, draft reply, and agent-job starts.
Chat first-run command rail hides automatically after a successful user or assistant message exists.
Chat resume-latest action now uses an explicit context summary explaining the restored session shell, message history, model context, and recovery state.
Chat workflow panel now exposes a Copy diagnostics action.
Chat route diagnostics include active session, friendly id, model, provider, connection state, message count, waiting/sending state, recovery state, and risky mutation gate without secrets.
Chat risky mutation gate is backed by the same flow-risk helper used by the workflow panel.
Chat diagnostics and command rail helpers have focused coverage for safe export, resume context, and first-run visibility.
Chat workflow controls keep command shortcuts in the workflow panel instead of adding more inline main-surface guidance.
Chat timeline classification, flow-risk detection, workflow summary, route diagnostics, resume-latest context, and command rail now live in a route-private workflow module.
Chat helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full chat route for summary coverage.
Chat route file is reduced from 3504 to 3215 lines while preserving current workflow panel, diagnostics, and first-run command behavior.
HermesWorld receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Files receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Files route now uses shared route error fallback instead of page reload fallback.
Files route now uses shared loading state.
Files now exposes a visible operation-status strip for refresh, download, rename, delete, folder creation, and diagnostics copy state.
Files safe diagnostics bundle includes root, current path, selected file metadata, filter state, last API error, counts, and health without file contents or secrets.
Files UI path-safety helpers block outside-workspace paths before rename, delete, download, or save operations proceed.
Files context-menu actions now expose disabled states and reasons for protected runtime/config files and unsafe paths.
Files now has a pinned-roots area for common workspace folders such as scripts, docs, routes, screens, and server.
Files now includes an Agent touched filter for agent, swarm, jobs, conductor, Hermes, and Codex file workflows.
Files file-list search now highlights matching filename text.
Files breadcrumb header remains sticky inside the browser panel while browsing deep folders.
Files live DOM check confirms pinned roots, diagnostics copy, agent filter, operation status, and no desktop horizontal overflow.
Files data contracts, path-safety helpers, filtering, health summaries, pinned roots, operation status, and safe diagnostics now live in a route-private workflow module.
Files helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full route component for path and diagnostics coverage.
Files route file is reduced from 2495 to 1939 lines while preserving the current tree, preview, edit, and diagnostics behavior.
Files remote workspace mode copy is asserted through exported workflow constants while the route still blocks local folder picker regressions.
Terminal receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Terminal route now uses shared route error fallback instead of page reload fallback.
Hidden terminal mounts no longer open PTY streams until the terminal route is visible.
Terminal now classifies active session state as connected, reconnecting, detached, failed, or read-only.
Terminal toolbar exposes a safe diagnostics copy action with session id, shell, cwd, transport, state, and close reason without secrets.
Terminal toolbar exposes Copy output with secret redaction and an explicit 8000-character truncation marker.
Terminal dangerous command detection is shared by paste confirmation and helper tests.
Terminal status strip now keeps current working directory, session state, and diagnostics in the first viewport.
Terminal copy-output and diagnostics controls are visible in desktop and mobile route DOM checks.
Terminal diagnostics helper keeps read-only and last-close fields ready for future PTY recovery work.
Daily habit state now uses typed storage validation.
History state now uses typed storage validation.
Existing helper tests cover habit rules, summaries, heatmap, and share reports.
75 tracker now has a quick mode that hides the embedded full tracker for mobile-first toggling.
75 tracker now shows streak-risk messaging based on remaining work and time of day.
75 tracker now stores per-habit recovery notes for incomplete items.
75 tracker reset requires explicit browser confirmation before clearing today.
75 tracker habit checkboxes and heatmap cells now expose accessible labels.
75 tracker route smoke now covers hard-mode quick state.
75 tracker custom habit templates now persist as a typed template list with add-template support.
75 tracker now has a modified/travel day setting that adjusts water and workout rules.
75 tracker weekly trend legend explains statuses without relying on color alone.
75 tracker first viewport includes a compact mobile summary with complete count, next habit, and streak risk.
Conductor receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Conductor first viewport now has a dominant quick mission launch surface with advanced controls kept behind the modal.
Conductor mission launch now shows a live readiness checklist for goal, cwd, model, tools, and approvals.
Conductor readiness chips use blocked, warning, and ready severity styling that matches the workspace status language.
Conductor now shows a CWD locked indicator when the mission scope has a project directory.
Conductor route diagnostics can be copied as a safe JSON bundle with phase, session key, stream state, worker counts, cwd, and error state.
Conductor portable-plan copy now previews goal, constraints, verification, handoff, readiness, and explicitly excludes secrets.
Conductor mission drafts now persist through versioned launch-draft storage with legacy goal-draft fallback.
Conductor modal readiness summary shares the same helper logic as the first-viewport launch flow.
Conductor launch draft contracts, Tyler recurring templates, readiness helpers, diagnostics, portable plan preview, and draft serialization now live in a route-private workflow module.
Conductor helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full route component for launch-readiness coverage.
Conductor route file is reduced from 4068 to 3791 lines while preserving current mission launch, diagnostics, portable plan, and readiness behavior.
Operations route now uses shared route error fallback instead of page reload fallback.
Operations route now uses shared loading state.
Operations first viewport now computes a single primary action from fleet health.
Operations fleet filters now include a first-class Needs Tyler view.
Operations health rows now support keyboard up/down navigation.
Operations route smoke now verifies opening the new-agent modal on desktop and mobile.
Ops Intelligence now classifies snapshot freshness as fresh, stale, or unknown.
Ops Intelligence findings now expose confidence labels: observed, inferred, stale, or needs live verification.
Ops Intelligence first viewport now shows source, generated time, and snapshot freshness chips.
Ops Intelligence now has a mobile at-a-glance panel for risks, blockers, and next action.
Changes Since Last Update is now collapsible after review.
Ops Intelligence remediation links now route to the likely fix page instead of always Tasks.
Ops Intelligence now includes a compact owner / system / risk / action table.
Barry first viewport now states its role as 1-on-1 prep and follow-through.
Barry first viewport now exposes the last interaction timestamp.
Barry primary CTA now chooses schedule, prep, follow-up, or copy-brief behavior from meeting state.
Barry mobile view now has a compact daily card with next meeting, prep, actions, and freshness.
Barry meeting cards now support ArrowUp and ArrowDown selection.
Swarm route now uses shared route error fallback instead of page reload fallback.
Swarm route now uses shared loading state.
Swarm2 route now uses shared route error fallback instead of page reload fallback.
Swarm2 route now uses shared loading state.
Swarm2 surface contracts, runtime data contracts, mission queue builders, and operator-state helpers now live in a route-private workflow module.
Swarm2 helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full route for operator-state coverage.
Swarm2 route file is reduced from 2657 to 2283 lines while preserving the current cards, runtime, reports, and router behavior.
Swarm2 shared text compaction, label cleanup, and relative-time helpers are reusable for future worker panels.
Memory receives a hidden route-level H1 from the shell.
Memory route now uses shared loading state.
Memory search panel now exposes source filters for all sources, Codex, Hermes, rollouts, skills, and workspace docs.
Memory file filtering now combines active/recent/stale/all tabs with filename search and source filters.
Memory diagnostics can be copied as a safe JSON bundle with query, source filter, selected path, API status, counts, stale count, and newest file without secrets.
Memory selected-file actions now include Use in current task with provenance, freshness, path, and bounded content context.
Memory source classification distinguishes rollout summaries, skills, Codex, Hermes, workspace docs, and generic memory.
Memory task context copy explicitly warns to verify drift-prone facts before using memory in the current task.
Memory first viewport keeps search dominant while exposing provenance, stale warning, source filters, diagnostics, and current-task handoff.
Skills cards now make installed, available, and recently used skills visually distinct.
Skills installed sort now supports a Last used mode backed by dashboard usage data.
Skills cards now show search-result snippets explaining why a skill matched.
Skills mutation buttons now expose install, remove, and toggle risk labels.
Skills route links now include MCP when a skill references MCP, tools, or servers.
Pinned favorite skills now surface as a first-viewport favorites section.
MCP first viewport now has All, Recently failed, Stale, and Auth attention filters.
MCP mobile view now shows compact server health cards with primary action and last error.
MCP now includes a capability matrix for tools, resources, prompts, auth, risk, action, and Skills handoff.
MCP servers now route to likely Skills searches based on server/tool names.
MCP helper coverage now verifies capability rows, primary actions, and Skills handoff routes.
Profiles first viewport now states that profiles affect Chat, LILY, agents, tools, routing, jobs, and workspace behavior.
Profiles now has an active-profile banner with quick links to Chat, Operations, Conductor, Jobs, and Settings when relevant.
Profiles mobile view now has compact active-state cards with top capabilities and quick activate controls.
Profile cards now expose validation badges for provider, model, tools, and routing with route links.
Profile cards now show used-by references for Chat, Operations, Conductor, Jobs, and Settings when validation fails.
Profiles now blocks deleting active and default profiles before the API call.
Profile details now show a description diff preview before saving edits.
Profile cards now include a copy-for-experiment action that opens the create flow from the selected profile.
Meetings review filters now include Today, This week, Missing notes, and Needs follow-up views.
Meetings local search now matches titles, attendees, action items, issues, decisions, status, owners, and priorities.
Meetings first viewport now exposes route diagnostics for selected id, detail loading state, and action state.
Meetings now shows prep-window CTAs when the next meeting starts within 24 hours.
Meetings mobile view now has an at-a-glance agenda with next meeting, prep, join, and open follow-ups.
Meeting cards now show attendee and owner chips for faster scanning.
Meetings To Do handoff now includes unresolved selected-meeting actions and dedupes carry-forward actions.
Meeting brief copy now supports attendee redaction by default.
Two-week meeting load now includes trend interpretation guidance.
Meetings workflow helpers and meeting data contracts now live in a route-private pure utility module.
Meetings helper tests now import pure workflow logic directly instead of loading the full route component.
Meetings route file is reduced from 2426 to 2057 lines while preserving current agenda, prep, filtering, and handoff behavior.
Meetings participant, unresolved-status, severity, filter, search, brief, and trend helpers are reusable for future panels.
Presence now has a single primary sync action based on auth, drift, stale devices, or healthy state.
Presence mobile view now has a compact glance card with availability, current activity or meeting, trust, sync, and copy actions.
Presence now exposes route diagnostics for Teams auth, last sync, and source errors.
Presence status can be copied as a short shareable availability summary.
Presence default mode persists across sessions for Graph-first, manual-first, or device-first operation.
Manual sync actions now include do-not-disturb quick presets for 30, 60, and 120 minutes.
Presence keyboard shortcuts now support sync and focus-state actions outside editable controls.
Presence primary action and mobile action show optimistic Syncing state while actions run.
Presence helper coverage now verifies primary actions, share text, diagnostics, default-mode normalization, and DND payloads.
IT Ops now has a mobile glance card for SLA and approval blockers.
IT Ops ticket filters now include Needs Tyler approval, SLA risk, Unassigned, and Touched by Tyler modes.
IT Ops tickets now show SLA risk chips, owner chips, native approval versus workspace-derived labels, and last-touched markers.
IT Ops ticket cards can deep-link to native ConnectWise tickets when the source provides a URL.

IT Ops helper coverage now verifies native ticket URLs, SLA labels, approval boundaries, copy templates, and safe diagnostics.
IT Ops action queue and header now keep native ConnectWise approval/workflow guidance visible in diagnostics-oriented copy.
IT Ops ticket list now supports ArrowUp/ArrowDown and j/k keyboard navigation outside editable controls.
IT Ops source freshness and last pull remain visible near the title with ConnectWise degraded and zero-ticket distinctions.
IT Ops mobile and desktop route smoke pass after queue, copy, filter, and diagnostics changes.
IT Ops keeps non-native Tasks handoff out of the PSA queue until a concrete native-safe follow-up path is available.

remainingMajorBuckets: Large screen splits above roughly 1500 lines: dashboard, phone, chat, playground, files, conductor, swarm2, meetings, settings.
New user workflows: meeting prep, task handoffs, profile diffing, MCP guided setup, ConnectWise deep links, swarm worker controls.
Advanced diagnostics: page-specific bundles, route fixtures, active-state visual smokes, live source drilldowns.
Mobile daily modes: driving/walking Phone Cockpit and compact Tasks.
Data and persistence migrations beyond the first typed-storage consumers.
Performance and virtualization work for chat messages, swarm logs, transcript timelines, and memory result lists.
Command palette and keyboard shortcut expansion across pages.
